sprint.name: Forge Expansion Sprint
sprint.note: A scheduled review is not a guaranteed release. The forge publishes at most one coherent change when it clears the quality and safety gates; otherwise it skips the window.
sprint.secondaryAutomation: A separate GitHub Actions workflow runs Tuesday and Friday at 15:17 UTC. It can only create or improve approved declarative-engine apps and records accepted publishes in this ledger.

method.label: Inspect
method.description: Review the live site, repository, current apps, open feedback, bugs, depth, accessibility, and portfolio gaps.

method.label: Compare
method.description: Consider at least three candidate changes and choose the one that creates the strongest useful depth or breadth.

method.label: Build one
method.description: Create or improve one coherent feature or app, including mobile, keyboard, touch, reduced-motion, teardown, and edge cases.

method.label: Validate
method.description: Run JavaScript syntax checks, feedback-routing tests, registry rules, required-file checks, and safety-boundary validation.

method.label: Publish or skip
method.description: Merge and deploy only after every required check passes. Skip when the candidate is weak, unsafe, duplicative, or incomplete.

qualityGates: One coherent change maximum per review window
No secrets, trackers, accounts, hidden storage, or unreviewed network behavior
No direct execution of user feedback
Mobile, keyboard, touch, focus, and reduced-motion behavior considered
Repository validation must pass before main is published

queue.title: Vertical logistics variants
queue.reason: Only expand elevator-dispatch play if future work adds distinct tower grammars, accessible route summaries, multi-car coordination, fire-service overrides, tenant equity tradeoffs, or richer destination batching rather than more call labels.

queue.title: Layout production variants
queue.reason: Only expand print-layout play if future work adds distinct plate geometries, drag previews, accessible page summaries, multi-client batches, print defects, or richer client-contract tradeoffs rather than more job labels.

queue.title: Care triage variants
queue.reason: Only expand diagnosis and care play if future work adds distinct symptom grammars, accessible patient summaries, multi-room staffing, owner communication contracts, or richer recovery ethics rather than more animal labels.

queue.title: Broadcast control variants
queue.reason: Only expand live-production play if future work adds distinct rundowns, accessible show recaps, multi-role booth coordination, timing pressure, or richer audience contracts rather than more camera labels.

queue.title: Power grid variants
queue.reason: Only expand microgrid play if future work adds distinct grid grammars, accessible outage summaries, multi-operator dispatch, neighborhood equity contracts, or richer renewable forecasts rather than more district labels.

queue.title: Astronomy scheduling variants
queue.reason: Only expand observing-window play if future work adds distinct sky grammars, accessible night summaries, multi-scope coordination, spectrum contracts, or richer weather and calibration tradeoffs rather than more target labels.

queue.title: Emergency dispatch variants
queue.reason: Only expand dispatch-routing play if future work adds distinct map grammars, tutorial calls, accessible route summaries, multi-ambulance coordination, hospital transfers, or richer mutual-aid tradeoffs rather than more incident labels.

queue.title: Audio memory variants
queue.reason: Only expand rhythm-memory play if future work adds distinct chart grammars, non-audio summaries, tutorial callouts, melody drafting, duet modes, or richer accessibility review paths rather than more channel labels.

queue.title: Stormwater simulation variants

queue.title: Greenhouse simulation variants

queue.title: Sonar deduction variants

queue.title: Physics aiming variants
queue.reason: Only expand physics-launch play if future work adds distinct surfaces, tutorial shots, accessibility review paths, wind fields, multiplayer targets, or richer trajectory planning rather than more asteroid labels.

recentDecisions.title: Lift Line 1.0.0
recentDecisions.summary: Added a standalone local vertical-logistics strategy game with seven-floor elevator dispatch, passenger queues, capacity limits, power draw, patience pressure, express routing, repair recovery, adaptive rush modes, session-only sky-lobby unlock, scoring, responsive DOM tower rendering, touch and keyboard controls, reduced-motion behavior, optional local audio, and teardown.
